CMDSDownloader

Contents

  • CMDSDownloader
    • CMDSDownloader.__init__()
    • CMDSDownloader.download()
    • CMDSDownloader.format_to_localtime()
    • CMDSDownloader.for_waves()
    • CMDSDownloader.for_winds()

CMDSDownloader#

class oceanicospy.retrievals.CMDSDownloader(dataset_id, variables, lon_min, lon_max, lat_min, lat_max, start_datetime_local, end_datetime_local, difference_to_UTC, output_path, file_format='netcdf')[source]#

Bases: object

Copernicus Marine Data Store (CMDS) downloader mirroring ERA5 input style. Includes an ERA5-like post-processing method to rewrite the NetCDF in LOCAL time (shift + crop).

Parameters:

  • dataset_id (str) – CMDS dataset identifier (case-sensitive, as listed in the CMDS catalog).

  • variables (List[str]) – Variables to request (dataset-specific names).

  • lon_min (float) – Bounding box in degrees (EPSG:4326).

  • lon_max (float) – Bounding box in degrees (EPSG:4326).

  • lat_min (float) – Bounding box in degrees (EPSG:4326).

  • lat_max (float) – Bounding box in degrees (EPSG:4326).

  • start_datetime_local (datetime) – Local-time window of interest.

  • end_datetime_local (datetime) – Local-time window of interest.

  • difference_to_UTC (float) – Local minus UTC (hours). Example: UTC-5 -> -5.

  • output_path (str) – Directory where the output will be written.

  • file_format (str) – “netcdf” or “zarr”. The post-process method supports only “netcdf”.

  • output_filename (Optional[str]) – File name to write inside output_path (e.g., “winds_CMDS.nc”).

__init__(dataset_id, variables, lon_min, lon_max, lat_min, lat_max, start_datetime_local, end_datetime_local, difference_to_UTC, output_path, file_format='netcdf')[source]#
Parameters:

  • dataset_id (str) – CMDS dataset identifier (case-sensitive, as listed in the CMDS catalog).

  • variables (List[str]) – Variables to request (dataset-specific names).

  • lon_min (float) – Bounding box in degrees (EPSG:4326).

  • lon_max (float) – Bounding box in degrees (EPSG:4326).

  • lat_min (float) – Bounding box in degrees (EPSG:4326).

  • lat_max (float) – Bounding box in degrees (EPSG:4326).

  • start_datetime_local (datetime) – Local-time window of interest.

  • end_datetime_local (datetime) – Local-time window of interest.

  • difference_to_UTC (float) – Local minus UTC (hours). Example: UTC-5 -> -5.

  • output_path (str) – Directory where the output will be written.

  • file_format (str) – “netcdf” or “zarr”. The post-process method supports only “netcdf”.

  • output_filename (Optional[str]) – File name to write inside output_path (e.g., “winds_CMDS.nc”).

download()[source]#

Execute CMDS subset request. Returns absolute path to the created resource.

Return type:

str

format_to_localtime()[source]#

Notes

  • Only supports NetCDF outputs. For Zarr, implement a separate workflow.

Return type:

None

classmethod for_waves(lon_min, lon_max, lat_min, lat_max, start_datetime_local, end_datetime_local, difference_to_UTC, output_path, file_format='netcdf')[source]#

  • lon_min (float)

  • lon_max (float)

  • lat_min (float)

  • lat_max (float)

  • start_datetime_local (datetime)

  • end_datetime_local (datetime)

  • difference_to_UTC (float)

  • output_path (str)

  • file_format (str)

Return type:

CMDSDownloader

classmethod for_winds(lon_min, lon_max, lat_min, lat_max, start_datetime_local, end_datetime_local, difference_to_UTC, output_path, file_format='netcdf')[source]#
